primary_flags.row_ref: ROW-8436-8438 adjacent (Nov 21-24 window); police report date Nov 22 2025
primary_flags.cross_rf: RF-02 (visit-refusal cascade anchor event; police report is RF-02 escalation context)
primary_flags.message_text: Nov 22 2025: No text messages exchanged (police report date gap in record). Context: Eli filed police report because Olivia refused court-ordered visit Nov 22. CP2 Q4 confirmed: Melissa told Olivia about the police report filing. Documented emotional impact: Olivia did not speak to Eli for over a week.
primary_flags.summary: Nov 22 2025: Melissa informed Olivia that Eli filed a police report related to her refusal to come for his court-ordered visit. CP2 Q4 confirmed: Olivia was home when the police phone call happened; Melissa told Olivia; Olivia was mad at Eli and stopped talking to him for over a week. RF-11 mechanism: parent involves child in the awareness of law-enforcement proceedings arising from the child's own court-ordered-visit refusal. The child becomes emotionally responsible for the legal consequence (Eli 'called the police on her'). This is documented by behavioral impact (week-long silent treatment toward Eli) rather than a text quote — no text messages were exchanged on Nov 22 (consistent with police report gap). Note per ADD-005: Eli believes text evidence of Melissa referencing the police report to Olivia may exist; flag this note for text-record search in B-029 ROW range. Sev-2: legal-proceedings exposure with documented behavioral impact on child. ADD-006 context: police-report disclosure is consistent with Olivia-as-stabilizer pattern — Melissa recruits Olivia's anger as a conflict tool. Compound COMP-RF11-003: RF-11+RF-02 (police report is RF-02 gatekeeping escalation context).

primary_flags.cross_rf: RF-02 (visit-refusal cascade; non-accountability positioning); RF-04 (COMP-RF04-028 'big contempt case' cluster)
primary_flags.message_text: ROW-14841 area: 'I did nothing wrong with her not going to your house because I didn\'t keep her from going. It was her choice not to go and I am not expected to forcibly remove her from the home'
primary_flags.summary: Nov 2025: Melissa explicitly frames Olivia's court-ordered visit refusal as Olivia's autonomous choice, positioning herself as non-accountable for enforcement. Text-documented: 'It was her choice not to go and I am not expected to forcibly remove her from the home.' RF-11 parentification mechanism per ADD-002: court-order compliance is presented to Olivia as Olivia's decision to make, not a legal requirement Melissa must enforce. The child bears the burden of deciding whether to comply with a court order. This is distinct from RF-02 (gatekeeping = parent preventing access): here Melissa explicitly transfers the non-compliance decision to the child while framing her own role as neutral. CP2 Q5 confirmed: Melissa has said this framing explicitly; Eli's view is that it is sometimes genuine child preference and sometimes Melissa-directed. The text-record evidence is the explicit 'it was her choice' statement. Sev-2: systematic framing (not isolated) + documented visit-refusal cascade across multiple weekends. ADD-006 context: placing court-compliance burden on Olivia is consistent with emotional-stabilizer parentification — Olivia must manage her own legal compliance while Melissa maintains deniability. Compound COMP-RF11-004: RF-11+RF-02+RF-04.
